meta.description: Maps intel topic tags to capabilities and downstream component categories

acquisition.label: Acquisition & Procurement
acquisition.description: Government and enterprise drone procurement programs driving component demand
acquisition.demand_signal: Each platform acquired pulls full BOM — frames, FCs, motors, ESCs, batteries, props, receivers, GPS. Bulk procurement amplifies demand across all categories.

counter-UAS.label: Counter-UAS / C-UAS
counter-UAS.description: Detection, tracking, and defeat of adversary drones
counter-UAS.demand_signal: C-UAS systems require RF sensors, directional antennas, mesh networking for distributed detection, and compute for AI-based classification.

autonomy.label: Autonomous Flight
autonomy.description: GPS-denied navigation, obstacle avoidance, mission planning without human intervention
autonomy.demand_signal: Autonomy stacks need high-compute SBCs (Jetson, QRB5165), LiDAR/depth sensors, IMUs, and advanced flight controllers with SLAM capability.

AI.label: AI / Machine Learning
AI.description: On-board inference for target recognition, terrain classification, decision-making
AI.demand_signal: AI workloads drive demand for GPU-equipped SBCs (Jetson Orin, Hailo-8), high-res cameras for training data, and thermal sensors for multi-modal fusion.

C2.label: Command & Control / Datalinks
C2.description: Ground control stations, telemetry links, beyond-line-of-sight comms
C2.demand_signal: C2 links need TX/RX pairs, directional antennas, mesh radios for relay, and encrypted video transmitters. BVLOS drives higher-power radio demand.

electronic warfare.label: Electronic Warfare / EW
electronic warfare.description: Jamming, spoofing, signal intelligence, spectrum denial
electronic warfare.demand_signal: EW systems share RF components with C2 but require wideband antennas, SDR-capable radios, and compute for real-time signal processing.

one-way attack.label: One-Way Attack / OWA
one-way attack.description: Loitering munitions and kamikaze FPV drones designed for single-use strikes
one-way attack.demand_signal: OWA drones are CONSUMED on use — every strike = full BOM replacement. Ukraine burns thousands/month. Drives highest volume demand of any category.

swarming.label: Swarm Operations
swarming.description: Coordinated multi-drone operations with distributed decision-making
swarming.demand_signal: Swarms multiply per-unit demand by swarm size (10-1000x). Each node needs mesh radio, compute, GPS. Software is the differentiator (Shield AI Hivemind, Anduril Lattice).

ISR.label: Intelligence, Surveillance, Reconnaissance
ISR.demand_signal: ISR platforms carry the most expensive payloads — thermal cameras ($3K+), LiDAR ($1K+), multi-spectral sensors. Long endurance drives battery demand.

FPV.label: First Person View
FPV.description: Pilot-in-the-loop drone operations via video feed, primarily for strike and racing
FPV.demand_signal: FPV is the FULL BOM — every component category is pulled. Ukraine FPV consumption is the single largest volume driver in the global drone component market.

attritable.label: Attritable / Expendable
attritable.description: Low-cost platforms designed to be lost without strategic impact
attritable.demand_signal: Attritable design philosophy drives demand toward cheapest-available components. STM32 over Jetson, ELRS over Silvus, 3D-printed frames over carbon fiber.

NDAA.label: NDAA Compliance
NDAA.description: National Defense Authorization Act supply chain requirements — no Chinese components
NDAA.demand_signal: NDAA compliance creates premium demand for US/allied-manufactured alternatives to Chinese components. Drives 2-5x price premium across all categories.

doctrine.label: Military Doctrine
doctrine.description: Evolving military concepts of operation for drone employment
doctrine.demand_signal: Doctrine changes (e.g., Army SRR program, CCA concept) drive long-term procurement patterns. Component demand follows doctrinal adoption 12-24 months later.

loitering munition.label: Loitering Munitions
loitering munition.description: Extended-endurance strike platforms that orbit before engaging
loitering munition.demand_signal: Loitering munitions need longer endurance than FPV OWA — drives demand for efficient motors, high-capacity batteries, and thermal sensors for target ID.

GPS-denied.label: GPS-Denied Navigation
GPS-denied.description: Navigation without satellite positioning — visual, inertial, terrain-matching
GPS-denied.demand_signal: GPS-denied ops are the #1 capability gap. Drives demand for high-compute SBCs, optical flow sensors, LiDAR, and advanced IMUs. Every serious military platform needs this.

Blue UAS.label: Blue UAS Cleared List
Blue UAS.description: DoD-approved platforms meeting cybersecurity, supply chain, and data security standards
Blue UAS.demand_signal: Blue UAS approval creates a procurement fast-track. Components in Blue UAS platforms see accelerated demand from government buyers. 49 platforms currently listed.

VTOL.label: Vertical Takeoff & Landing
VTOL.description: Fixed-wing aircraft with vertical takeoff capability — hybrid designs
VTOL.demand_signal: VTOL platforms need both multirotor AND fixed-wing components — dual motor sets, transition mechanisms, larger batteries for hover phase.

mesh network.label: Mesh Networking
mesh network.description: Ad-hoc radio networks for multi-drone and drone-to-ground comms
mesh network.demand_signal: Mesh radios (Silvus, Doodle Labs, Persistent Systems) are the most supply-constrained component class — 20-22 week lead times, allocated to DoD programs.
